overview: The Village of Weston Plan Commission approved the site plan for the new Mountain Bay Metropolitan Police Department and Municipal Court facility at 3600 Community Center Drive, subject to staff conditions. The commission also approved a salt storage shed inspection program for wellhead protection districts, establishing twice-yearly inspections to protect the village's drinking water supply.

agenda.item: Call to order and roll call
Site plan review for Mountain Bay Metro Police Department and Municipal Court facility at 3600 Community Center Drive
Creation of salt storage shed inspection program for Wellhead Protection District
April 2026 staff approved certified survey map and site plan
Planning and development department monthly project update report
Future agenda items and staff discussion

discussions.item: Site plan review for Mountain Bay Metro Police Department and Municipal Court facility
discussions.body: Staff presented the site plan for the new police department and municipal court at 3600 Community Center Drive. Chief Jeremy explained challenges including a gas pipeline easement that required rotating the building orientation to face east. The building is designed for 50-60 officer capacity for future growth beyond current 40 officers and 4 clerical staff. An alternate storage shed was discussed, with Commissioner Zagami suggesting relocating it further north to accommodate future building expansion. The landscaping meets current relaxed code standards. Motion to approve was made and passed unanimously with conditions 2-5 as listed by staff.

discussions.item: Salt storage shed inspection program for Wellhead Protection District
discussions.body: Staff proposed a twice-annual inspection program for salt storage facilities within the wellhead protection district - a warm weather inspection for structural integrity and a winter operational inspection for material handling practices. Staff noted one existing facility near Sternberg that never went through the wellhead protection conditional use process and commissioners agreed it should be brought into compliance. Discussion emphasized the village's vulnerability due to shallow wells (90-110 ft) and sandy soil. Motion to approve passed unanimously, with amendment to report back to both Plan Commission and Public Works Committee.
Motion to approve minutes as presented was made, seconded, and passed unanimously without discussion.

discussions.item: April 2026 staff approved certified survey map and site plan
Planning and development department monthly project update
discussions.body: Staff announced the first joint Plan Commission and Village Board steering committee meeting for the comprehensive plan is scheduled for Monday, June 1st at 5:00 p.m. Staff will send calendar invites.

Electric vehicle charging stations will be added to a future agenda, likely in the fall. Discussion arose about potential residential TIF districts for workforce housing. Staff mentioned Ehlers has prepared a presentation on residential TIF qualifications that could be shared with commissioners, possibly integrated into comprehensive plan discussions.

publicComment: Jim Pensson of 5002 Arrow Street, Weston, spoke via online connection. He asked the commission to exercise common sense regarding landscaping standards for the police department project, referencing previous relaxation of requirements for the Greenheck Turner building. He also requested that the salt shed inspection program include annual reporting on visits and findings to ensure accountability.
actionItems: Site plan for Mountain Bay Metro Police Department and Municipal Court facility at 3600 Community Center Drive approved with staff conditions 2-5
Salt storage shed inspection program approved for implementation in wellhead protection district
Staff to report inspection findings to both Plan Commission and Public Works Committee next year
Staff to reach out to existing salt storage facility on Sternberg that has not gone through wellhead protection conditional use process
Joint Plan Commission and Village Board steering committee meeting scheduled for June 1st at 5:00 p.m.
Staff to send calendar invite for June 1st steering committee meeting
Electric vehicle charging stations to be added to future agenda in fall
Staff to share Ehlers presentation on residential TIF qualifications with commissioners
title: Plan Commission
